extra.reasoning:
The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on every reachable execution.

Key observations:
1. opted_out is monotonic: it starts false, becomes true only via t1 (on optout), and NO transition ever sets it back to false. Once true, always true.
2. The only transition that ever sets sent := true is t2, whose guard is "not opted_out". So sent can only be raised to true while opted_out is false.
3. Every transition that can fire while opted_out is true sets sent to false or leaves it unchanged:
   - t1 (optout): sets sent := false (and is the very entry into opted_out=true).
   - On send_attempt with opted_out=true, t2 is guard-disabled, so only t3 fires: sent := false.
   - t4 (tick): sets sent := false.
Therefore the state (opted_out=true, sent=true) is unreachable. The entry into opted_out=true (t1) sets sent=false, and from then on no enabled transition can ever set sent back to true. ticks is irrelevant to the property and bounded in [0,8]. Hence in every reachable state, opted_out true implies sent false, for executions of any length (including up to 12 events).
